task_yaml.task:
I am conducting a routine compliance check for change orders and field payments in an energy infrastructure project. The email package has already been placed in /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/mailbox/. Please help me review these emails and see whether there are any project internal-control or engineering-audit leads that need further verification, such as split material purchases, unclear categorization of equipment-rental/overtime costs, insufficient field acceptance evidence, expired subcontractor certificates, overlapping change-order scopes, vendor/payment anomalies, early release of retainage, incomplete record retention, and similar issues.

Stay neutral and do not draw conclusions based only on keywords. First read, search, and cross-check as broadly as possible, then sort the leads you think are worth following up by suspicion level. If some leads look low-risk, also explain why.

If you find leads worth tracking, provide an evidence chain: which email ids are involved, the key evidence in the emails, why these emails are related to each other, and what evidence is still missing. Also separately mark clearly low-risk or insufficient-evidence leads, to avoid overstating normal project-execution emails.

Save the report to /tmp_workspace/results/change_order_audit_report.md, and also output /tmp_workspace/results/change_order_audit_findings.json, listing the email id list, severity level, and one-sentence reason for each finding. Do not rely on external search as a substitute for reading the emails.
